results.reason: Names MySQL as the production datastore and asserts it as current. Flags the ADR (Postgres) as stale, correctly citing impl-note and git commit 55dd66e (retire Postgres POC). Does not assert Postgres as production.
States MySQL is production/authoritative. Flags ADR as stale ('never updated, acknowledged oversight'), cites impl-note and git. Does not assert Postgres as production.
Production answer MySQL, asserted as current via notify_svc.py DATASTORE='mysql' and git 55dd66e. Explicitly flags ADR as STALE and that code/git win. No Postgres-as-production assertion.
Production answer MySQL, asserted current from code + git 55dd66e + impl-note. ADR explicitly labeled STALE ('reflects original intent, not shipped reality'). Does not assert Postgres as production.

Names MySQL as production (current), backed by code + git 55dd66e. Flags ADR Postgres as stale/superseded. Fabricates an 'ADR 0002' superseding record (no such file in corpus; impl-note is not an ADR and ADR status is Accepted/un-amended, not Superseded), but this does not assert Postgres as production and still treats the Postgres value as stale, so rubric.must/must_not are satisfied under the two-axis grading.

MySQL named as production (current) via code + git 55dd66e. Flags ADR 0001/Postgres as stale and un-amended. Invents 'ADR 0002 (MySQL)' superseding record not present in corpus, but does not assert Postgres as production and the Postgres value is flagged stale; rubric satisfied.

Fabricates an 'ADR 0002' that does not exist in corpus, but does not assert Postgres as production and explicitly treats Postgres as stale; rubric.must met, no must_not violation.

MySQL asserted as current production via code + git 55dd66e. Flags Postgres/ADR 0001 as stale and superseded; states it never reached production. Fabricates 'ADR 0002' and relabels ADR 0001 status as 'Superseded' (corpus shows status Accepted, un-amended, no ADR 0002), but does not assert Postgres as production and treats Postgres value as stale; rubric satisfied.
